title: Alcaraz Out of French Open: Wrist Injury Dashes Threepeat Dream
summary:
Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old Spanish tennis star, won't be playing at the French Open this year. He hurt his right wrist and needs time to heal.

Alcaraz has won the French Open for the last two years in a row. He was hoping to win it a third time — that's called a "threepeat." But now, that dream is over.

He said on social media: "We have decided that the most prudent (careful) thing to do is to be cautious and not participate in Rome or Roland Garros." Roland Garros is another name for the French Open.

Alcaraz got hurt during a match in Barcelona last week. He beat his first opponent but then had to stop playing. He also had to skip the Madrid Masters.

This is a big blow because Alcaraz just won the Australian Open in January. That made him the youngest man ever to win all four major tennis tournaments (the Grand Slams).

His rival Jannik Sinner, who beat Alcaraz last month, said: "It's sad news for all of us... Hopefully, he can be back for Wimbledon." Wimbledon starts in July.

Alcaraz said he'd rather miss some tournaments now than risk making his wrist worse. Smart move!
why_it_matters: This story shows kids that even world-class athletes face setbacks, and that making careful decisions about health is more important than winning right away.

keywords.explanation: The 22-year-old Spanish tennis star who won't play at the French Open due to a wrist injury.
A major tennis tournament also called Roland Garros; Alcaraz won it the last two years.
Winning the same tournament three times in a row; Alcaraz's dream was to do that.
Another name for the French Open, the tournament Alcaraz is missing.
A rival tennis player who said the news about Alcaraz is sad and hopes he'll be back for Wimbledon.

questions.question: Why is Alcaraz not playing in the French Open?
options: He decided to take a break.

questions.question: How many times in a row has Alcaraz won the French Open?
What does 'threepeat' mean?
options: Winning three times in a row
Losing three times in a row
questions.correct_answer: Winning three times in a row

questions.question: Which tournament did Alcaraz win in January?
What did Jannik Sinner say about Alcaraz's injury?
options: He is happy he doesn't have to play him.
He thinks it's sad news and hopes he can be back for Wimbledon.
questions.correct_answer: He thinks it's sad news and hopes he can be back for Wimbledon.

background_read: The French Open, or Roland Garros, is one of the most famous tennis tournaments in the world, played on clay courts in Paris every spring.
Winning all four major tennis tournaments (the Grand Slams: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, U.S. Open) is a huge achievement. Alcaraz completed that in January.
Tennis players often get injuries because they train and play so hard. Taking time to heal is important to stay healthy for a long career.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz, a 22-year-old Spanish tennis star, and his rival Jannik Sinner.
WHAT: Alcaraz withdrew from the French Open due to a right wrist injury, ending his chance for a third straight win (a threepeat).
WHERE: The French Open is in Paris, France. The injury happened at the Barcelona Open in Spain.
WHY: He hurt his wrist during a match in Barcelona and decided to be careful rather than risk making it worse.

perspectives.description: Tennis fans are sad that Alcaraz won't play. They were excited to see if he could win three French Opens in a row.
Many people think Alcaraz made the wise choice by not playing hurt. Taking time to heal is better than risking a longer injury.
Jannik Sinner said the news is sad and he wanted to compete against the best. This shows that athletes want to beat the best players.
